description_long: This is a medium-sized wooden barrel with a polygonal, slightly tapered shape and reinforced by two metallic bands, one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood staves are vertically aligned and have a warm brown color, with visible grain and subtle wear, giving the barrel a rustic appearance. The top is open, revealing a reddish liquid inside, suggesting it is used for storage or transport of fluids such as wine or juice. The metallic bands are a dull gray, providing structural support and a visual contrast to the wood. The rim of the barrel is uneven, with a few notched or chipped sections, adding to its aged, utilitarian look. There are no visible handles, lids, or markings, and the interior appears hollow and filled with liquid.
description: A rustic wooden barrel with metal bands, open at the top and filled with a reddish liquid. The barrel has a polygonal shape, visible wood grain, and no handles or markings.
description_view_0: Front view of the barrel showing a full, intact rim and the reddish liquid inside.
description_view_1: This view highlights a large notch or chip in the rim, exposing more of the liquid.
description_view_2: The barrel is seen with a smaller notch in the rim, and the metal bands are clearly visible.
description_view_3: A prominent chipped section on the rim is visible, with the liquid inside and the wood grain apparent.
attribute_banding_or_reinforcement: metal hoops at top and bottom
